title: Siddhivinayak Temple, Siddhatek
tagline: Siddhivinayak Temple, Siddhatek — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: The Siddhivinayak Temple of Siddhatek is a Hindu temple dedicated to Ganesha, the elephant-headed god of wisdom.
overview.description: The Siddhivinayak Temple of Siddhatek is a Hindu temple dedicated to Ganesha, the elephant-headed god of wisdom. The temple third of the Ashtavinayaka, the eight self formed (swayambu) shrines of Ganesha in the Indian state of Maharashtra and the only Ashtavinayaka shrine in Ahmednagar district.

topPlaces.description: Pedgaon is a panchayat village in the state of Maharashtra, India. Administratively, Pedgaon falls under the Shrigonda Taluka of Ahmadnagar District, Maharashtra.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Siddhivinayak Temple, Siddhatek?
faq.a: The best time to visit Siddhivinayak Temple, Siddhatek is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Siddhivinayak Temple, Siddhatek?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (86 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (90 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Siddhivinayak Temple, Siddhatek?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Siddhivinayak Temple, Siddhatek at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Siddhivinayak Temple, Siddhatek?
faq.a: Stays in Siddhivinayak Temple, Siddhatek start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Siddhivinayak Temple, Siddhatek?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pedgaon, Ahmednagar.
